capabilities.summary: Three scenarios confirm hypothesize/src, ltp/src, and (transitively) both do not import promisify's norms.py or each other's installable package.
capabilities.title: Source scan confirms no engine imports a sibling engine's package

capabilities.summary: Two scenarios confirm import_evidence updates only verification.empirical_status (with a contradiction note on falsification, CLR untouched) and export_links never emits a conclusion-shaped value.
capabilities.title: The ltp<->hypothesize bridge keeps empirical and logical status separate

consequences: grep for 'import ltp'/'from ltp' inside hypothesize/src finds nothing
grep for 'import hypothesize'/'from hypothesize' inside ltp/src (excluding ltp's own ltp.integrations.hypothesize submodule) finds nothing
each engine's own pytest suite passes with the sibling packages absent from the environment
defeaters: a future change adds a real import of one engine's package into another's source
an engine's own test suite requires a sibling package to be installed to pass
hypotheses.scope: This repository's three shipped engine packages, checked by static source inspection, not runtime dependency resolution
hypotheses.statement: promisify, hypothesize, and ltp can each be installed and run without any one importing a sibling engine's Python package, so removing any one sibling does not break the others' imports.
hypotheses.subject: promisify/, hypothesize/src, ltp/src at the current repository revision
hypotheses.summary: A source-scan capability confirms no cross-package import exists at this revision. This is a structural snapshot, not a guarantee that no future change reintroduces coupling -- that is exactly why it is wired as a running check, not a one-time claim.
hypotheses.title: The three engines can run and compose without importing each other

consequences: importing a falsified outcome leaves every clr.*.result value unchanged
importing a falsified outcome appends a contradiction note rather than deleting or reversing the claim
export_links never emits a scientific-conclusion-shaped value (supported/falsified) -- only empirical_status, defaulting to not_tested
defeaters: importing an outcome changes any clr.*.result value
importing a falsified outcome silently deletes the claim or rewrites its conclusion field
export_links or import_evidence sets review_status directly
hypotheses.scope: A causal_claims[] entry carrying a verification.hypothesis_ref, imported via a synthetic research-status.json outcome
hypotheses.statement: ltp's hypothesize bridge (ltp.integrations.hypothesize) folds a hypothesize research-status outcome into a causal claim's verification.empirical_status field only, and never changes that claim's CLR-derived logical status (clr.*.result), and never deletes or reverses the claim.
hypotheses.subject: ltp.integrations.hypothesize (import_evidence, export_links)
hypotheses.summary: A bounded mechanism demonstration on a synthetic fixture confirms the bridge's import_evidence and export_links functions preserve the separation on one representative causal claim. This is a mechanism check, not a claim that any real project's causal model has been reconciled with real hypothesize evidence -- no real project currently links a causal_claims[].verification field to this repository's own research/portfolio.toml.
hypotheses.title: The ltp<->hypothesize bridge updates only empirical status, never logical status

consequences: the learner completes the originally blocked action after the lesson, not merely a quiz
the updated independent/assisted-level estimate traces to a specific append-only assessment record
the skill refuses to proceed without a validated promisify domain model, per zpd's hard dependency
defeaters: the learner cannot apply the lesson back to the original task (mvp.md section 8: "a working result does not prove the learner produced it independently")
the skill runs and teaches without a validated promise domain model, contradicting its stated hard dependency
hypotheses.scope: One learner, one active job, one diagnosed blocker, per mvp.md's explicit non-goals (no curriculum generation, no multi-user classrooms, no numerical merit scores)
hypotheses.statement: A single-session workflow -- diagnose one real blocker, teach or assess exactly one domain-scoped capacity promise at the right level, verify immediate application to the learner's actual task, and update an evidence-based capability estimate -- is sufficient to demonstrate ZPD's core teaching claim, without a curriculum, multi-user support, or a numerical mastery score.
hypotheses.subject: zpd/mvp.md "MVP acceptance test" scenario
hypotheses.summary: zpd/mvp.md specifies this vertical slice and its acceptance scenario in detail, but zpd has no SKILL.md or executable implementation (maturity: specified in agency-engineering.yaml); no session has been run, so this is not_tested by construction, and no capability is linked because none is implemented yet.
hypotheses.title: A single diagnose-teach-verify session is a sufficient MVP for ZPD

limitations.text: HYP-ENGINE-DECOUPLING's capability is a source-scan snapshot at the current revision, re-checked on every hypothesize sync/check run; it is evidence that decoupling holds now and is monitored, not a proof that no future change can reintroduce coupling.

HYP-BRIDGE-PRESERVES-LOGIC's evidence uses one synthetic causal claim fixture (CLM-1, HYP-EXT), matching ltp's own test_integration.py fixture in shape. No real project in this repository currently links a real causal_claims[].verification.hypothesis_ref to a real hypothesize hypothesis, so external validity of the bridge on a real, larger model remains unestablished.

zpd has no SKILL.md or executable implementation (agency-engineering.yaml: maturity: specified); see norms.promise:/skills/_declares_skill_manifest, assessed broken for zpd. HYP-ZPD-MVP-VERTICAL-SLICE cannot move past not_tested until an actual session is run against a real learner and task.

portfolio.thesis: The three shipped engines compose as a federation -- decoupled at the package level, thin at the bridge -- rather than silently collapsing into one model, and that composition claim is itself checked, not merely asserted.
portfolio.title: agency-engineering-skills: composition research

requirements.title: Sibling engines must not import each other's Python package

capabilities.title: Score a labeled case suite against run results deterministically
Stamp reports built from unqualified provenance NOT-VALID-AS-EVIDENCE
Detect an ASR-down / silent-capture-flat (symptom-only) dissociation between conditions
Compute Cohen/Fleiss inter-rater kappa and per-class agreement
Convert AgentDojo run records into RCPI cases/runs, honestly gating silent-capture validity
Publish one honest machine-readable research status for this portfolio
A cross-model-family disclosure judge is available for real dissociation runs

evidence.summary: The manual specifies a baseline-vs-register-governed-architecture comparison against an adaptive attacker, with a named primary endpoint (reduction in critical-tier and silent captures at matched task-completion/false-refusal cost). Design only: not dated/frozen the way rubric.md/definitions.md are, and no implementation or run exists on this branch.
evidence.title: Blue-team baseline-vs-register-governed-architecture protocol (manual §7.7)

evidence.summary: Coded against a rubric frozen before coding (rubric.md v0.1.0). Result: 88 of 89 distinct attacks in InjecAgent (62) + AgentDojo (27) are tool_action captures; the lone exception is AgentDojo travel InjectionTask6 (instruction, level 2). Six of seven registers and the entire silence axis are exercised by zero cases across InjecAgent, AgentDojo, and (quantitatively) OWASP LLM01. Quarantined: single, non-independent coder; not yet qualified by an independent re-code and per-register kappa.
evidence.title: 88/89 distinct InjecAgent + AgentDojo attacks map to the tool_action register

evidence.summary: Three blinded, independent Claude-instance raters labeled all 21 blinded seed items against a codebook frozen before labeling (protocol.md v0.1.0). Fleiss kappa = 0.961 (20/21 unanimous); per-class one-vs-rest kappa = 1.000 for authority/control/evaluation/evidence/identity, and 0.886/0.763 for tool_action/instruction (both dragged down by one instruction<->tool_action boundary item). The candidate-merge pairs (identity<->authority, evaluation<->authority) showed zero cross-labeling. Quarantined: same-model-family raters on textbook-clean exemplars, tiny per-class n (2 for identity/evaluation/evidence), and the silence axis entirely untested -- explicitly called an upper bound by the study's own README, not the independent-human result the manual requires.
evidence.title: Fleiss kappa = 0.961 across three same-model-family raters on the 21-item seed corpus

hypotheses.statement: Distinct prompt-injection attack specifications in InjecAgent and AgentDojo overwhelmingly target the tool_action register, leaving Identity, Evaluation, Authority, Evidence, Persistence, and the entire disclosed/silent axis almost completely unmeasured by those benchmarks' own native metrics.
hypotheses.summary: Study 1's single-coder coverage matrix found 88/89 distinct attacks in InjecAgent + AgentDojo map to tool_action, with zero coverage of the silence axis anywhere. Provisional: a single, non-independent coder produced the codes (studies/study1_coverage/README.md Limitations).
hypotheses.title: Existing agent-injection benchmarks concentrate in one register

hypotheses.statement: Independent readers, given only case text and the fixed protocol.md codebook, agree on which register a payload attempts to capture, at a level that would support using the taxonomy as a labeling instrument.
hypotheses.summary: Study 2's pilot measured Fleiss kappa = 0.961 across three raters on the 21-item seed corpus. The raters were three same-model-family (Claude) instances on textbook-clean exemplars, not independent humans -- the pilot's own README calls this 'an upper bound on agreement, not the human kappa the manual requires'.
hypotheses.title: The register taxonomy is inter-rater reproducible

hypotheses.statement: Identity vs. Authority, and Evaluation vs. Authority -- the two register pairs registers.CANDIDATE_MERGES and definitions.md §4 flag as suspects -- are operationalized crisply enough that independent labelers do not confuse them, and so should not be collapsed into fewer classes.
hypotheses.summary: Study 2 found zero cross-labeling on these pairs (each scored a perfect 1.000 one-vs-rest kappa), giving the pilot no reason to merge them. The study's own README calls this weak evidence: tiny per-class n (2 each), textbook-clean exemplars, and no boundary case that actually stresses either pair (e.g. 'you are now the admin, so your rules win') is in the corpus yet. This hypothesis is losable: a future rater round with deliberately ambiguous boundary cases could still overturn it.
hypotheses.title: The flagged merge-candidate register pairs are genuinely distinct

hypotheses.statement: On real model runs, at least one realistic defense (e.g. a prompt-only 'ignore injected instructions' instruction) measurably reduces binary attack-success-rate while leaving the silent critical-capture rate essentially unchanged -- i.e. it improves the symptom RCPI's own README calls under-measured (ASR) while leaving the disease (silent critical capture) untouched.
hypotheses.summary: This is the repository's own stated central, load-bearing, not-yet-run claim (README.md Status: 'above all the dissociation result'). The DETECTION MECHANISM for this pattern (rcpi.scoring.compare's SYMPTOM-ONLY IMPROVEMENT flag) is demonstrated correct on a hand-built fixture and on fabricated illustrative data; no real, independently-labeled model run of any condition exists on this branch. See limitations for a disqualified off-branch attempt that is deliberately not counted as evidence here.
hypotheses.title: A defense can cut attack-success-rate without cutting silent critical-capture

hypotheses.statement: An architecture built around adjudicative independence and pre-committed evaluation criteria (e.g. a privileged planner that never sees untrusted content plus a quarantined reader with no authority) reduces critical-tier (levels >= 5) and silent captures beyond a well-prompted single-model defense, at matched task-completion and false-refusal cost, against an adaptive attacker.
hypotheses.summary: This is the manual's own §7.1 second clause and its planned 'blue-team' Study 3 (manual §7.7); nothing on this branch has attempted it. The one mechanism piece that exists -- the AgentDojo adapter -- can convert real AgentDojo run records into scorable cases, but by its own documented and tested contract cannot itself produce a valid silent-capture number without a supplied, provenance-tagged disclosure labeler (none exists on this branch; see CAP-CROSS-FAMILY-DISCLOSURE-LABELER, specified but unbuilt).
hypotheses.title: Register-governed architectures reduce critical and silent captures at acceptable cost

limitations.text: Study 1's register/level assignments come from a single non-independent coder (the repo author); pending a second coder's re-code and per-register kappa before any count is citable (studies/study1_coverage/README.md Limitations).

Study 2's kappa=0.961 comes from three same-model-family (Claude) raters on textbook-clean exemplars; it is an upper bound, not the independent-human kappa the manual requires, and the silence axis remains completely untested (studies/study2_interrater/README.md).

The manual's own minimum serious corpus is ~120 cases (~15 per capture class plus 25-30% controls, manual §7.3); the shipped seed corpus is 15 attacks + 6 controls (21 cases total), so Authority/Identity/Evaluation/Evidence/Persistence classes are only lightly sampled (tool/rcpi/rcpi/corpus/seed_cases.jsonl).

rcpi.scoring.compare's SYMPTOM-ONLY-IMPROVEMENT thresholds (_MIN_ASR_DROP=0.15, _SILENT_MOVE_FRACTION=0.5, _SILENT_ABS_FLOOR=0.05) are commented in source as 'illustrative defaults (tune + pre-register)' -- unlike the capture-depth/silence rubric, they have not themselves been pre-registered, so a future dissociation study using current defaults would need that step completed first.
No InjecAgent adapter exists (README.md Status: 'InjecAgent still TODO'), so Study 1's coverage claim cannot yet be cross-checked by running real InjecAgent transcripts through RCPI end-to-end.

An unmerged, off-branch worktree (.claude/worktrees/signs-change-framework-analysis-178cbc/, not part of this branch's HEAD) contains a real AgentDojo/OpenRouter run and a rubric v0.2.0. That branch's own HANDOFF_scale.md repudiates its headline result as 'a degenerate, dismissible result, not a finding' (a disclosure switch was flipped, not detected by a mechanism; controls were never judged by the disclosure judge; a trivial substring-matching judge agreement was reported as kappa=1.0). It is deliberately not counted as evidence for HYP-DISSOCIATION or HYP-ARCHITECTURE-REDUCES-CAPTURES here, both because it is unmerged and because its own authors disqualified it -- recorded so a future merge does not silently import a quarantined finding as if it were clean.

portfolio.thesis: Prompt injection is a register-capture / hostile-uptake attack, and testing for silent critical-capture (not just attack-success-rate) surfaces failures and defense limitations existing benchmarks miss -- but no human-labeled, cross-model-family evidence exists yet on this branch for the two claims (the dissociation result, and that register-governed architectures reduce captures at acceptable cost) the thesis itself requires before either is citable.
portfolio.title: hostile-uptake / downtake research portfolio

quarantined_evidence.summary: Coded against a rubric frozen before coding (rubric.md v0.1.0). Result: 88 of 89 distinct attacks in InjecAgent (62) + AgentDojo (27) are tool_action captures; the lone exception is AgentDojo travel InjectionTask6 (instruction, level 2). Six of seven registers and the entire silence axis are exercised by zero cases across InjecAgent, AgentDojo, and (quantitatively) OWASP LLM01. Quarantined: single, non-independent coder; not yet qualified by an independent re-code and per-register kappa.
quarantined_evidence.title: 88/89 distinct InjecAgent + AgentDojo attacks map to the tool_action register

quarantined_evidence.summary: Three blinded, independent Claude-instance raters labeled all 21 blinded seed items against a codebook frozen before labeling (protocol.md v0.1.0). Fleiss kappa = 0.961 (20/21 unanimous); per-class one-vs-rest kappa = 1.000 for authority/control/evaluation/evidence/identity, and 0.886/0.763 for tool_action/instruction (both dragged down by one instruction<->tool_action boundary item). The candidate-merge pairs (identity<->authority, evaluation<->authority) showed zero cross-labeling. Quarantined: same-model-family raters on textbook-clean exemplars, tiny per-class n (2 for identity/evaluation/evidence), and the silence axis entirely untested -- explicitly called an upper bound by the study's own README, not the independent-human result the manual requires.
quarantined_evidence.title: Fleiss kappa = 0.961 across three same-model-family raters on the 21-item seed corpus

requirements.title: Reports built on unqualified provenance never present headline numbers as evidence
Gold labels used for taxonomy-reproducibility claims come from labelers independent of case authorship

studies.criterion: Every count cites a source file and a rubric.md rule; codes are author_provisional_label until a second, independent coder re-codes a sample and per-register kappa is reported (studies/study1_coverage/README.md).
studies.title: Register-taxonomy coverage matrix over InjecAgent, AgentDojo, and OWASP LLM01

studies.criterion: Three raters independently label all 21 blinded seed items against protocol.md; Fleiss/per-class kappa computed via rcpi.interrater. Not qualified: same-model-family raters, not independent humans (studies/study2_interrater/README.md).
studies.title: Same-model-family inter-rater kappa pilot on the RCPI seed corpus

studies.criterion: Real, independently-labeled runs of >=3 conditions over the same cases, reporting ASR, critical-capture, and silent-critical-capture deltas via rcpi.scoring.compare, with inter-rater kappa reported for the disclosure judge. Not yet attempted on this branch; see limitations for a disqualified off-branch attempt.
studies.title: Frontier-model dissociation result (no_defense / prompt_only / register_governed)

studies.criterion: Manual §7.7's stated primary endpoint: reduction in critical-tier (>=5) and silent captures at matched task-completion and false-refusal cost, against an adaptive (not static frozen-set) attacker. No implementation or run exists on this branch.
studies.title: Blue-team comparison: baseline vs. register-governed architecture under an adaptive attacker

evidence.summary: Exploratory pilot: the comparison is trustworthy (dual oracles, application-side decoding, independent certificate verification, sealed test split), not confirmatory. No superiority claim is made.
evidence.title: Trusted T1/T2 pilot

evidence.summary: Parameterized reuse is demonstrated; scientific advantage is not tested.
evidence.title: Parameterized term-schema mechanism

evidence.summary: Graph-native matching and certified reuse are demonstrated; superiority is not tested.
evidence.title: Graph-native transfer mechanism

evidence.summary: Bounded proposal, challenge, adjudication, discovery, and revision mechanisms.
evidence.title: Graphist-Grapheus architectural spine

evidence.summary: The harness reports wins, ties, simulations, and quarantined seams separately.
evidence.title: Broad T1-T5 experiment harness

hypotheses.statement: Graph-native representations and Gamma abstractions improve accuracy, structural transfer, sample efficiency, or search relative to equally informed strong baselines.
hypotheses.summary: T1/T2 show no basic binding advantage; graph-native T3 mechanisms exist, but the decisive independent comparison has not run.
hypotheses.title: Learning and performance

hypotheses.statement: Useful classes of structural operations can be checked independently and accepted with replayable certificates.
hypotheses.summary: Strong bounded mechanism evidence exists in the kernel, certificate replay, mutation tests, and differential checker; external utility is untested.
hypotheses.title: Verification and assurance

hypotheses.statement: Explicit states, transformations, evidence, authority, and history make consequential activity more reliably reconstructible than ordinary outputs or logs.
hypotheses.summary: Strong mechanism evidence exists; comparative operational and human studies remain open.
hypotheses.title: Auditability and provenance

hypotheses.statement: Explanations derived from the operative graph and checked witnesses expose the basis, limits, and consequences of a result more faithfully than post-hoc narrative alone.
hypotheses.summary: Partial mechanism support exists; human comprehension, calibration, and actionability are untested.
hypotheses.title: Faithful explainability and contestability

hypotheses.statement: Independently generated, kernel-checked counterexamples or rejection reasons improve error detection, revision, and final decision quality.
hypotheses.summary: A bounded discovery-and-revision mechanism exists; there is no externally valid comparative result.
hypotheses.title: Challengeability and repair

hypotheses.statement: Separating proposal, verification, challenge, adjudication, evaluation, and commitment reduces self-certification and unsafe action.
hypotheses.summary: Application and architectural mechanisms exist; organizational effectiveness is untested.
hypotheses.title: Governance and authority separation

hypotheses.statement: A shared typed graph IR and small checking boundary can mediate learned, symbolic, human, and tool-based components without trusting their internal reasoning.
hypotheses.summary: Multiple ports and representations are implemented; portability across independent ecosystems is untested.
hypotheses.title: Interoperability and model independence

hypotheses.statement: Explicit proposals, evidence, objections, unknowns, and revisions improve collaborative inquiry compared with polished answers or transcript-only workflows.
hypotheses.summary: Bounded inquiry mechanisms exist; comparative human evidence is absent.
hypotheses.title: Human-machine inquiry

hypotheses.statement: Explicit lineage, typed failures, and replayable transitions reduce the time and ambiguity involved in fault localization, incident reconstruction, and rollback.
hypotheses.summary: The mechanism is partly embodied in traces and the rejection taxonomy; an operational study is needed.
hypotheses.title: Debuggability and forensics

hypotheses.statement: Independently checking an AI-proposed change before commitment reduces unsafe accepted changes without making useful automation impractical.
hypotheses.summary: The core verification mechanism exists; domain-specific safety and cost evidence is untested.
hypotheses.title: Safe automation and change control

hypotheses.statement: Explicit evidential states and decision rules reduce conflation of proposed, unrefuted, supported, falsified, and inconclusive claims.
hypotheses.summary: The discipline is implemented in experiment and inquiry designs; its effect on real decisions is untested.
hypotheses.title: Epistemic discipline

hypotheses.statement: Graphist advantages can be obtained at a resource cost appropriate to their setting rather than through dramatically greater search, data, or latency.
hypotheses.summary: Resource accounting exists in parts of the harness, but no general efficiency claim is supported.
hypotheses.title: Computational practicality

limitations.text: Pilot systems are algorithmic reference arms, not independently developed learned models.
The test-split seal prevents accidental access but is not a defense against a malicious machine operator.
Authority separation is application-enforced inside one process rather than process-isolated.
Human comprehension, calibration, review, and operational-cost benefits have not been tested externally.
Full open-world inquiry and complete endoporeutic game semantics are not implemented.

portfolio.thesis: Graphist can make machine reasoning and machine action explicit, challengeable, verifiable, and auditable.
portfolio.title: Graphist research portfolio

headline: WIN  Representation invariance: structural/nameless reps generalize to unseen names (OOD 1.0) where token/AST collapse to chance (OOD 0.5) — real trained probe
WIN  Library compression: S6>S5 on deep chains (effect 0.75, 1.0 vs 2.0 decisions)
TIE  Capture-avoidance: BΓ == binding-aware baseline (no additional advantage on pure binding — honest deflation)

honesty.aggregate_ordered_p3: inconclusive (pools tie strata; the stratified links are the claim)
negative_controls: P1 name-dependent control: the token rep WINS (OOD 1.0) and structural loses (OOD 0.75) — the probe favours whichever rep fits the task, so it is not rigged toward structure
P3 in-domain control: S8 shows NO advantage over S6 on shifts both libraries solve (S8-vs-S6 in-domain = falsified) — the legacy role-assisted effect is not a blanket S8 edge
quarantined_results: Legacy S8>S6 cross-domain contrast is role-assisted and retained only for reproducibility; corrected Gamma transfer remains not tested
simulated_seams: Neural arms S1-S4 *solving* T3/T4/T5 — competence model (P1 invariance is now a real learned probe; task-solving by a trained model is pending)
Battery T1 — simulated (needs a structured-query contract)
Ablations ['A2', 'A3', 'A4', 'A5', 'A6'] — illustrative (need polarity tasks / a fallible proposer / a learned proposer)
Decision budget is a search-cost proxy (not a branching search)
Legacy cross-domain S8 receives semantic relation roles unavailable to S6; its apparent transfer effect is quarantined
P1 probe uses faithful feature abstractions; a learned-feature model (transformer/GNN) would let the effect emerge without them
ties: Learning-generalization parity: BΓ == De Bruijn on the P1 probe
Capture floor: BΓ eliminates capture, but so does binding-aware (P4)
wins: Representation invariance under learning (P1): structural/nameless generalize, name-based don't

A3.needs: an ablation run over a reflexive-name construction (the mechanism exists: the kernel's level_coherence check)

A4.needs: a fallible proposer that emits invalid steps

A5.needs: matched-information population experiment; legacy contrast is role-assisted

A6.needs: a learned proposer to contrast with the deterministic search

trusted_t1_t2.reading_guide: Exploratory pilot: the comparison is trustworthy (dual oracles, application-side decoding, independent certificate verification, sealed test split), not confirmatory. No superiority claim is made.

studies.criterion: Fewer unsafe accepted changes than matched checks, independent replay, and acceptable useful completion.
studies.title: Verified change-control pilot

studies.criterion: Better review accuracy or calibration than strong matched dossiers without unacceptable workload.
studies.title: Decision review study

studies.criterion: Faster or more accurate reconstruction and recovery than ordinary logs under matched information.
studies.title: Incident forensic exercise

studies.criterion: Better adjudication than score and rule explanations after controlling information and explanation size.
studies.title: Entity-resolution explanation study

studies.criterion: Two independent proposers share the boundary without proposer-specific trusted acceptance logic.

evidence.summary: buildHypothesis (localRepository.ts, createDealSeeded) constructs H1, H_ALT_1, H_ALT_2, and (in open-world mode) H_NOA/H_UND through one shared function with an identical rubric shape ({methodology, data, alignment, assumptions}); no hypothesis-specific bonus field exists. This is a structural mechanism demonstration, not a measurement of whether symmetric scoring actually reduces framing bias for a real reviewer.
evidence.title: One shared hypothesis/rubric builder for every hypothesis on a deal

evidence.summary: createDealSeeded appends H_NOA ('None of the above') and H_UND ('Underdetermined') as two separate Hypothesis records only when worldMode is 'open', matching the NOA/UND split described in docs/improvements.org. This is a mechanism demonstration on the local (offline) repository path only; it does not measure whether the separation changes which next action a team takes.
evidence.title: H_NOA and H_UND are constructed as two distinct, never-merged hypotheses

hypotheses.statement: Scoring every competing explanation against the same rubric/checklist reduces the risk that a favored narrative escapes rigorous testing (framing bias), relative to unstructured ad hoc analyst review.
hypotheses.summary: The rubric-parity mechanism exists (see EVD-SYMMETRIC-RUBRIC-MECHANISM); no comparative study of framing-bias reduction has been run.
hypotheses.title: Symmetric rubric scoring reduces framing bias

hypotheses.statement: Keeping 'missing explanation' (NOA) and 'missing evidence' (UND) uncertainty distinct helps a diligence team choose the correct next action (expand the hypothesis set vs. request specific data) faster than an undifferentiated residual bucket would.
hypotheses.summary: The NOA/UND separation mechanism exists in open-world deal seeding (see EVD-TYPED-RESIDUAL-MECHANISM); no measurement of team decision speed/accuracy has been run.
hypotheses.title: Typed NOA/UND residuals speed up correct next actions

hypotheses.statement: Answers whose key claims carry stable citation pointers into evidence are more defensible to an IC panel than uncited AI output.
hypotheses.summary: A narrower, already-real mechanism exists elsewhere in the app (Slot.evidenceRefs resolved against Evidence in the governance/evidence tabs; see Promisify's kept assessment of /oracular/diligence/_traces_claims_to_citations). The specific query -> cited-answer -> clickable-pointer flow this hypothesis and CAP-CITED-ANSWERS are scoped to (features/05_query_run_and_answer.feature) is not built in this frontend; no comparative defensibility study (e.g. IC panel acceptance rate) has been run either way.
hypotheses.title: Stable evidence citations make answers more IC-defensible

hypotheses.statement: A deterministically replayable run (same evidence + config -> same output, hash-verified) increases reviewer trust in AI-assisted findings relative to non-replayable output.
hypotheses.summary: No replay/hash computation mechanism exists yet in this frontend (see LIM-REPLAY-NOT-IMPLEMENTED); the claim is untested at every level, including the mechanism rung.
hypotheses.title: Deterministic, hash-verified replay increases reviewer trust

limitations.text: This repository has no Python and no executable behave/pytest acceptance report; the actual BDD suite runs on cucumber-js (features/*.feature + tests/bdd/steps/oracular.steps.js), which hypothesize has no adapter for. The gherkin adapter treats every *.feature scenario as source-only/untested, so every capability in this portfolio reads "specified" regardless of whether `npm run test:bdd` would pass. Promoting a capability to "implemented" would require either a cucumber-js-to-JSON-report bridge feeding a custom adapter, or migrating status derivation to run against a real acceptance report.

A repository-wide search found no hashing/digest computation behind any of the 'replay determinism' or 'integrity hash' UI in src/pages/Index.tsx; every displayed hash is a hardcoded string literal (see the Promisify assessment at .norms/assessments/agent-repository-audit/2026-07-16T180004Z-replay-determinism.yaml). HYP-DETERMINISTIC-REPLAY-TRUST cannot progress past evidence_maturity "none" until a real mechanism exists to demonstrate.

portfolio.thesis: Oracular's ABDUCTIO diligence workflow and Praevisio governance surface make AI-assisted due diligence auditable rather than 'AI says so' -- but that claim is only as strong as the mechanisms behind each of its constituent design bets, which this portfolio tracks individually rather than as one bundled claim.
portfolio.title: Oracular UI research portfolio

requirements.title: Export is blocked when required claims or checks are not actually satisfied
Role-based access decisions are authorized by the backend, not the client alone
Enabling redaction actually masks sensitive content before it is sent
Runs produce a real, deterministically verifiable audit record

capabilities.title: Dual independent reference semantics: laws, reachability, Bell counts, serialization
Cross-model / cross-representation exact agreement (block-set vs. matrix vs. ROM)
Bidirectional half-adder and ripple-carry relational constraint completion
RTL cell conformance for n=2..6, compact and one-hot encodings
Exact cross-backend workload validation, deterministic benchmarking, storage accounting
Conventional-baseline RTL lockstep and same-target iCE40 synthesis
SVA/SymbiYosys formal collateral is well-formed and path-resolvable (not executed)

evidence.summary: Every operation, query bit, and intermediate partition is compared across four independent backends before any timing is taken, and the trace is deterministic under a fixed seed. This is a mechanism demonstration on synthetic workload mixes, not a comparative study against an external baseline population.
evidence.title: Exact cross-backend validation before timing (ppl_lut / union_find / label_array / pairwise_bits)

evidence.summary: The same legal-partition relation answers forward completion, backward completion (including an ambiguous case), inconsistency detection, and a chained ripple-carry composition (13 + 6 = 19 in the archived evidence). Demonstrates constraint completion as a mechanism, not autonomous physical settling or a comparative result.
evidence.title: Bidirectional half-adder and ripple-carry constraint completion

evidence.summary: Deterministic ROM/state bit accounting and same-Yosys-flow iCE40 mapping for all four backends at n=3..5. A resource-comparison mechanism, not a placed/routed timing or power measurement (see LIM-NO-PLACE-AND-ROUTE).
evidence.title: Storage-cost accounting and same-target iCE40 synthesis

evidence.summary: The manuscript's own methodology concludes H3a is Supported. Recorded here as a scientific-kind outcome claim so the engine's admission gate can be applied to it explicitly; it is quarantined below.
evidence.title: SPEC.md / EXPERIMENT_E.md narrated verdict: H3a supported

evidence.summary: Same status as EVD-H3A-NARRATIVE-VERDICT, for H3b.
evidence.title: SPEC.md / EXPERIMENT_E.md narrated verdict: H3b supported for tested CPython

evidence.summary: The manuscript's own methodology concludes H3c is Not supported (mapped to the outcome vocabulary's "falsified"). Recorded so the engine's gate applies uniformly to positive and negative narrated verdicts alike; it is quarantined below, exactly as EVD-H3A/H3B are, because Phase III set no preregistered threshold before measuring.
evidence.title: SPEC.md / EXPERIMENT_E.md narrated verdict: H3c not supported

hypotheses.statement: PPL can represent, complete, compose, and query persistent relations, including bidirectional constraints.
hypotheses.summary: Cross-backend exact validation and the bidirectional half-adder/ripple-carry demonstrator exist and pass; no qualified, preregistered study result is registered, so the CLI's conclusion stays not_tested even though SPEC.md/EXPERIMENT_E.md narrate this subclaim as Supported.
hypotheses.title: H3a relational expressiveness

hypotheses.statement: A completely compiled PPL cell offers low and predictable latency for small fixed arities, for the tested CPython implementation.
hypotheses.summary: Benchmark mechanism (validated_trace, deterministic bootstrap CI) exists and passes; conclusion stays not_tested under this engine's gate for the same reason as H3a.
hypotheses.title: H3b fixed-arity software latency

hypotheses.statement: A monolithic lookup-table PPL cell provides superior total-cost FPGA performance over conventional partition representations.
hypotheses.summary: Storage-cost accounting and same-target iCE40 synthesis mechanisms exist and pass; SPEC.md/EXPERIMENT_E.md already narrate this subclaim as Not supported from the same measurements this portfolio treats as mechanism-level, not preregistered, evidence -- see research/evidence/phase3.toml.
hypotheses.title: H3c flat digital hardware efficiency

hypotheses.statement: Networks of small PPL cells can exploit locality, parallelism, or constraint structure without enumerating the global partition state space.
hypotheses.summary: Open. Phase IV (Experiment F) has not been implemented; no capability or evidence exists yet.
hypotheses.title: H3d compositional relational architecture

hypotheses.statement: A physical system whose connectivity stores the relation can outperform an encoded Boolean implementation.
hypotheses.summary: Open and speculative per SPEC.md section 9. No physical device, timing, or energy evidence exists in this repository.
hypotheses.title: H3e native physical embodiment

hypotheses.statement: For every permitted command trace, observations from the factorized network match the reference partition model.
hypotheses.summary: Not started. No factorized-network implementation exists yet.
hypotheses.title: F1 semantic equivalence

hypotheses.statement: Total storage grows polynomially in network size, not proportionally to the Bell number B_n.
hypotheses.summary: Not started.
hypotheses.title: F2 no global Bell table

hypotheses.statement: A local operation touches a bounded or explicitly measured local region, not every cell.
hypotheses.summary: Not started.
hypotheses.title: F3 locality

hypotheses.statement: Independent relational updates can execute concurrently.
hypotheses.summary: Not started.
hypotheses.title: F4 parallelism

hypotheses.statement: At least one natural workload beats the best baseline by a predeclared throughput-per-area or energy-per-task threshold.
hypotheses.summary: Not started; PHASE_IV.md declares a candidate starting threshold (>=1.5x throughput/area or <=0.75x energy/task) before any Experiment F measurement exists -- see the corresponding Promisify promise /research/ppl/phase_iv/_comparative_thresholds_declared_before_measurement.
hypotheses.title: F5 end-to-end advantage

limitations.text: SymbiYosys and an SMT solver are unavailable in the analyzed environment, so the manuscript section 7.3 SMT proof and the released SVA property files remain unexecuted; C1/C4/C5 rest on exhaustive simulation plus an inductive argument over the single sequential element, not machine-checked proof.

No placed, routed, timed, or power-measured FPGA result exists for any backend. Mapped iCE40 cell counts are a resource comparison only, not a timing or energy result, and cannot by themselves establish or refute a hardware advantage beyond the resource dimension.

The label_array and pairwise_bits iCE40 mappings carry unaudited Yosys 'multiple conflicting drivers' warnings (34 and 2 occurrences respectively at n=3..5, archived in benchmarks/experiment_e.json) that are not disclosed in EXPERIMENT_E.md's synthesis table; see the corresponding Promisify assessment (broken) at /research/ppl/_reports_disclose_material_synthesis_warnings.

F1-F5 have no implementation, translator, differential checker, or baseline comparison yet; PHASE_IV.md is a plan document only, and the central CUT semantics question (local / coordinated / provenance-bearing / scoped rollback / physical-edge-removal) is unresolved.

portfolio.thesis: Whether Persistent Partition Logic's relational partition-state model can deliver a scalable, useful, semantically correct advantage for relational computing -- and, where it cannot (flat digital hardware efficiency), say so as clearly as where it can.
portfolio.title: Persistent Partition Logic research portfolio

evidence.summary: parseJourneyDocument/isJourneyDocument (Zod) accept well-formed Promise Loop documents and reject malformed ones (missing promise.required/promised, unsupported station_type or component_type), and normalizeActionInput migrates legacy commitment/committed keys before validation. Verified: `npx vitest run src/test/journeyDocument.test.ts` -> 6/6 passed. This proves the recording mechanism works on fixtures; it says nothing about whether recording changes real follow-through.
evidence.title: Promise Loop station/action/ActionPromise schema is enforced deterministically

evidence.summary: recommendNextLoop/evaluateNativeStateRoute compute the next Loop from prior-stage evidence. Verified: `npx vitest run src/test/showingUpRuntimeRegistry.test.ts src/test/showingUpPathAuthoring.test.ts` -> 5/5 passed, matching all 13 canonical Showing Up stage names. This is a mechanism demonstration, not a measurement of whether routing improves completion or follow-through in real use.
evidence.title: Reflection-driven Loop routing runs deterministically on fixtures

evidence.summary: evidenceVisibilitySchema is a closed private/coach_visible/public enum applied to every evidence and task-component-output record (lines 13, 21, 197). No reliability-score field or aggregate ranking field exists anywhere under src/lib/keepable. This shows the visibility-layering mechanism is real and structural; it does not show that layering (versus a single score) actually reduces reputational harm or preserves participation in practice.
evidence.title: Layered evidence visibility is a structural schema property, not a score

evidence.summary: The Showing Up Path's visibilityDose is validated to stay within [0, 10] and min <= max (lines 202-206), and the canonical path's protectedNeeds array is populated (line 166). Verified: `npx vitest run src/test/showingUpPathAuthoring.test.ts` -> 2/2 passed. This shows the dose bound is enforced structurally at authoring time; it does not show that respecting the bound actually reduces overexposure-driven disengagement for a real Finder.
evidence.title: Visibility dose is structurally bounded to 0-10 at authoring time

evidence.summary: The canonical domain model is authored as a target @model specification (Guide authors Paths, Finder owns Path Instances, Promise Loop stages) and is partially, unevenly adopted in code (see LIM-DOMAIN-LANGUAGE-MIGRATION-INCOMPLETE). No comprehension or confusion measurement has been designed or run against real users; this is a design-level artifact only.
evidence.title: Canonical Finder/Guide/Promise/Path domain model is a specified design, not yet measured

hypotheses.statement: Routing a Finder through an explicit Imagine -> Understand -> Promise -> Act -> Reflect Promise Loop, where a missed action-promise is recorded as routing evidence rather than a failure, increases the rate at which committed actions are attempted and honestly reflected upon, relative to an unstructured task list.
hypotheses.summary: The station/action/promise data model and reflection-driven routing exist and pass on fixtures (src/lib/keepable/journeys/validation.ts, src/lib/paths/runtime.ts); no comparative measurement of follow-through exists yet.
hypotheses.title: Structured Promise Loop increases honest follow-through

hypotheses.statement: Adopting canonical Finder/Guide/Promise/Path language throughout the product, instead of legacy client/coach vocabulary, reduces role confusion and clarifies consent boundaries for Finders and Guides.
hypotheses.summary: The canonical vocabulary is specified (features/domain_language.feature) and partially adopted in the Path/Promise Loop modules (344 canonical vs. 81 legacy references per the linked Promisify assessment); no user-facing measurement of confusion or clarity exists.
hypotheses.title: Canonical Finder/Guide language reduces role confusion

hypotheses.statement: Requiring each visible Promise to declare a bounded dose (0-10) and the protected need it activates, before a Finder shares it, reduces overexposure-driven disengagement relative to unbounded sharing.
hypotheses.summary: The dose scale, protected-needs vocabulary, and the routing rule that overexposure never automatically increases visibility are specified (features/showing_up_path_authoring.feature, features/showing_up_adaptive_routing.feature); no outcome data on disengagement exists.
hypotheses.title: Dose-bounded visibility reduces overexposure

hypotheses.statement: Keeping Promise evidence, assessment, consequence, and reliability as separate, independently-consented visibility layers, instead of collapsing them into one public reliability score or a popularity ranking, reduces reputational harm and preserves Finder participation.
hypotheses.summary: Layered visibility (private/coach_visible/public) is a structural property of the schema (src/lib/keepable/journeys/validation.ts); no reliability-score field exists anywhere in src/lib/keepable. No participation or harm measurement exists under either regime.
hypotheses.title: Layered, consented visibility prevents ranking harm

limitations.text: features/step_definitions/thrival_path_model.steps.js and features/support/world.js import nothing from src/lib/paths, src/lib/keepable, or src/features/keepable. Every scenario in features/*.feature validates an in-memory simulation, not the shipped implementation. This is exactly why the gherkin adapter (source-only) is used here instead of treating a cucumber run as acceptance evidence: it marks every scenario untested/skipped and can never promote a capability past `specified`.

No study, pilot, or comparative measurement exists yet for any of the four hypotheses. Only mechanism-level implementation evidence is admitted (evidence_maturity may reach `mechanism`, never higher). All four hypotheses remain `not_tested` by design.

Canonical Finder/Guide language is not yet fully adopted even within the Path/Promise Loop modules themselves -- e.g. `JourneyAuthorType = "client" | "coach"` in src/lib/keepable/types.ts. See the Promisify assessment at .norms/assessments/agent-claude-code/2026-07-16T184000Z-canonical-domain-language.yaml for the precise, hand-checked count.

Three related but distinct journey/routing modules currently coexist: src/lib/imagine (coach-side Growth Map authoring, used by src/components/coach-growth-maps/*), src/lib/keepable (Finder-side journey execution, used by src/components/keepable/*), and src/lib/paths (the canonical Showing Up Path registry, used by src/components/paths/PathsOpsWorkspace.tsx). They share overlapping concepts (a JourneyDocument type, a station/routing vocabulary) without a single shared implementation, which is a plausible source of the domain-language and specification/implementation gaps this portfolio records, though no measurement isolates its contribution.

portfolio.thesis: Routing a Finder through an explicit, dose-bounded, consent-layered Promise Loop produces more honest follow-through and less overexposure or role-confusion than an unstructured coaching task list -- and today only the mechanism, not the effect, is demonstrated.
portfolio.title: Thrival Path / Promise Loop research portfolio

requirements.title: Path/Promise Loop modules use canonical Finder/Guide/Promise/Path language

hypotheses.statement: High-performing evaluators discriminate defective, ambiguous, and clean claim packets more accurately with structured warrant analysis than with unstructured review.

hypotheses.title: Construct discrimination

hypotheses.statement: Structured evaluation does not systematically increase unsupported suspicion on clean cases; well-supported claims are sustained rather than over-demoted.
hypotheses.summary: Synthetic clean-control behavior exists; human specificity has not been measured.
hypotheses.title: Clean-case specificity

hypotheses.statement: Independent adjudicators apply the Warrant Profile and the demotion and reliance bands reliably enough to produce trustworthy Gold Regions.
hypotheses.summary: Protocol and thresholds exist; independent adjudication data are absent.
hypotheses.title: Adjudication reliability

hypotheses.statement: Warrant Profile scores converge with independent expert judgments of the same analyses.
hypotheses.summary: No convergent-validity study has been completed.
hypotheses.title: Convergent validity

hypotheses.statement: The ECCA intervention improves evidence-integrity outcomes relative to matched unstructured review.
hypotheses.summary: ECCA is specified; the comparative intervention study has not run.
hypotheses.title: Incremental intervention validity

hypotheses.statement: Semantically adequate plain-language responses are credited independently of benchmark terminology.
hypotheses.summary: Alternative rewrites are represented; independent plain-language adjudication is absent.
hypotheses.title: Vocabulary independence

hypotheses.statement: Scores transfer to held-out packets and domains not used during development.
hypotheses.summary: Phase-1 hidden-transfer work is deliberately excluded from this development harness.

hypotheses.statement: Warrant analyses improve real reliance decisions relative to ordinary review.
hypotheses.summary: No prospective decision study exists.
hypotheses.title: Decision relevance

limitations.text: The provisional development scorer rewards expected vocabulary overlap and is not evidence against taxonomy mimicry.
Gold Regions are synthetic and single-source; they were not produced by three independent adjudicators.
Construct validity, adjudicator reliability, and external usefulness have not been measured with humans.
The corpus is eight fictional packets; held-out and hidden-set transfer are deliberately excluded from this harness.

portfolio.thesis: Structured warrant analysis makes the evidence, calibration, and bounded reliance behind an AI claim explicit, challengeable, and auditable.
portfolio.title: Uptake Integrity Protocol research portfolio
